lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: It's mine. You can't have it, we found it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you found?

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Not dragonstones. I like dragonstones, that's why I came here. However, I found a valuable coin instead, it speaks to me... but you can't have it! It's precious to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want it, I have something better.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: What is it? Is it dragonstones? I like dragonstones!

lines.speaker: Olbertus (possessed)
lines.text: Ask it for five! It looks rich!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Five? Will it give us five?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I don't have five dragonstones.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Well then we are not interested!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I have five dragonstones, tell you what, I'll trade you them for that coin you found.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Yes! Yes! We'll trade it for the dragonstones.

lines.speaker: Olbertus (possessed)
lines.text: No we will not! It is too precious!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: But why? I like dragonstones...

lines.speaker: Olbertus (possessed)
lines.text: It is trying to trick us! Evil, nasty creature it is. Do not listen to it!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: But it seems nice enough...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am standing right here, you know? You really are crazy. Yes, I'll give you five! Do we have a deal or not?

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Yes! Yes!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Thanks for helping me, I'm not sure what would have happened if I had continued to listen to the voices in my head.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I took that coin from the Stone Relief in the Crypt of the Moon and trapped my friend Eodan in a secret room behind the relief. Could you return the coin to the relief and set him free?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thoughts, I'll keep it for myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Since it's valuable, I think I'll just just kill you for it.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Please! No!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Foolish decision!

lines.speaker: Shaeded Beast
lines.text: I require this one alive human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just kidding!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're crazy!

sections.title: If the player traded for the coin

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Thanks for helping me, I'm not sure what would have happened if I had continued to listen to the voices in my head.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I took that coin from the Stone Relief in the Crypt of the Moon and trapped my friend Eodan in a secret room behind the relief. Could you return the coin to the relief and set him free?

sections.title: If the player chose to fight Shaeded Beast

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Thanks for killing that creature.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: It appears that when I stole the coin, the creature possessed me.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I took that coin from the Stone Relief in the Crypt of the Moon and trapped my friend Eodan in a secret room behind the relief. Could you return the coin to the relief and set him free?

sections.title: If the player traded for the coin

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I'm glad I no longer have that coin. I'm not sure what would have happened if I had continued to listen to the voices in my head.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: For some reason the coin returned to me. Here, take it before it corrupts me again!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I took that coin from the Stone Relief in the Crypt of the Moon and trapped my friend Eodan in a secret room behind the relief. Could you return the coin to the relief and set him free?

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: Thanks for killing that creature.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: It appears that when I stole the coin, the creature possessed me.

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: For some reason the coin returned to me. Here, take it before it corrupts me again!

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I took that coin from the Stone Relief in the Crypt of the Moon and trapped my friend Eodan in a secret room behind the relief. Could you return the coin to the relief and set him free?

lines.speaker: Olbertus
lines.text: I'm glad I no longer have that coin. I'm not sure what would have happened if I had continued to listen the voices in my head.
